$comment: CONTRACT EXTENSION for demo-editing.html, layered on top of assets/demo/layers.json (which stays the canonical map/server contract — this file never overrides it). Server side this page expects the SCRATCH layer seeded 2026-06-12 on demo.honua.io: service 'maui-inspections' (internal/OData layer id 12, table public.maui_inspections, pk id, Point/4326) published with AccessPolicy AllowAnonymous + AllowAnonymousWrite, discoverable in GET /odata/Layers by Name == odata.layerName. The table carries CHECK constraints mirroring 'categories'/'statuses'/'noteMaxLength' below, so the sandbox blast radius is one synthetic table with enum-validated rows. WRITE LANE: the page never ships a credential — it probes the capability manifest and lights live writes only when capabilities[id == capabilities.editCapabilityId].available is true. As of seeding, honua-server gates ALL protocol writes behind the Pro entitlement 'editing.feature-edits' (#1548/#1552) and demo.honua.io runs Community → writes stay local-optimistic until a license lands; the page then lights up with no code change.

odata.$etagComment: OData responses support optimistic concurrency via @odata.etag / If-Match (412 on mismatch), but two live-server gaps keep this page from sending If-Match: the CORS preflight allow-headers list omits If-Match, and the single-entity GET (the etag source) 500s under the AOT build. Both reported upstream; PATCH without If-Match is last-write-wins, which is acceptable for a synthetic sandbox.

geoservices.$comment: The same layer on the GeoServices surface — documented alternative read path. Esri FeatureServer applyEdits (the Pro editing surface) is deliberately NOT used by this page; open-protocol OData CRUD is the story.

map.$comment: Framed on Maui island — the inspection points span Kapalua to Hāna.
